name: Ably Realtime Messaging API
what_it_does: Delivers realtime pub/sub messaging with channel presence, message history, and guaranteed ordering via a globally distributed edge network.
use_cases: Stream agent progress updates and intermediate results to a frontend UI in real time over a persistent channel
Broadcast state change events from an orchestrator agent to multiple subscribed worker agents simultaneously
Use channel presence to track which agent instances are currently online and available for work assignment
Retrieve recent message history on a channel so a newly started agent can catch up on missed events
Push low-latency notifications from a backend agent to mobile or web clients without polling
not_for: Reliable webhook delivery to customer HTTP endpoints (use Svix instead)
Persistent job queues with durable at-least-once processing guarantees (use SQS or BullMQ)
Large binary file transfers or video streaming (use a CDN or media-specific service)
best_when: You need low-latency, globally distributed pub/sub with presence awareness and message history for real-time agent-to-client or agent-to-agent communication.
avoid_when: Your use case is fire-and-forget webhook delivery to customer HTTP endpoints rather than persistent channel-based messaging.

auth.notes: API keys support capability restrictions (publish, subscribe, presence, history) per channel namespace. Token requests allow server-side issuance of short-lived scoped tokens for client-side use — recommended pattern for agents issuing tokens to untrusted clients.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 6 million messages/month, 200 peak connections, 200 peak channels
pricing.paid_tiers: Standard ~$29/mo, Pro ~$149/mo, Enterprise custom; scales on message volume and concurrent connections
pricing.notes: Free tier is suitable for development and low-traffic production. Message volume and connection limits are the primary upgrade drivers.

performance.rate_limits: Default publish rate: 50 messages/sec per channel; REST API: varies by plan; connection limits vary by plan tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SDK surfaces typed error codes with descriptive messages. Connection state machine events allow agents to react to disconnects and reconnects programmatically. REST API returns standard HTTP status codes with JSON bodies.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Ably assigns unique message IDs; duplicate detection is available within a short deduplication window. No client-supplied idempotency key on publish.
known_agent_gotchas: Token authentication requires a server-side token request endpoint; agents that issue tokens to clients must implement and host this endpoint themselves
Message history is limited by retention period (2 minutes on free, up to 72 hours on paid) — agents cannot retrieve arbitrarily old messages without a database backup
Channel occupancy and metrics events require explicit activation via channel params; not enabled by default
Presence enter/leave events are not guaranteed to arrive in order on high-churn channels — agents should not rely on presence alone for strict membership accounting
Publishing via REST API does not use a persistent connection; for high-frequency publishing, agents should use the persistent SDK connection to avoid connection overhead

security_score_components.security_notes: Capability restrictions on API keys and tokens limit what each key can do per channel. Token requests enable server-controlled access delegation. TLS enforced on all transports.
